reasoning: The VXVCLS index was at 20.07 on 2026-01-30. Volatility indices are typically mean-reverting and positively skewed (spiking rapidly during shocks and decaying slowly). The FRED statistical trend analysis yields a 37% probability of an increase by early February, which aligns well with the expectation of short-term mean reversion. Over a slightly longer horizon (March 3), there is more time for potential new market shocks to induce volatility spikes, which pulls the probability closer to 0.5 (43%). Uncertainty naturally increases with the forecast horizon, justifying the adjustment toward a coin toss.
